skill.name: api-testing-observability-api-mock
skill.description: Design realistic mock API services for development, testing, contract validation, demos, and performance simulations.
skill.summary: Build realistic mock APIs with stubs, scenarios, fixtures, validation, and test integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high findings, but review found no confirmed malicious intent or prompt injection. Most external command and weak crypto hits are false positives from markdown fences, ordinary description fields, and local mock examples. The skill should publish with warnings because generated mock servers can record request headers and bodies, and documentation examples include local network services and third-party documentation assets.

medium_findings.title: Generated Mock Servers May Capture Sensitive Request Data
medium_findings.description: The playbook examples record request headers and request bodies for verification. This is legitimate for API mocks, but it can store Authorization headers, cookies, or personal data if users point real clients or production traffic at the mock.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code explicitly copies headers and request bodies into mock tracking data. The risk depends on how users run generated mocks, so it is a moderate data handling concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings align with markdown code fences and references to the implementation playbook. The only meaningful command-style example is a local Docker healthcheck using curl against localhost.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows documentation references and fenced examples, not hidden runtime command execution. The curl command targets a local mock health endpoint.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak crypto matches occur on ordinary fields such as description strings and test names. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, encryption, password hashing, or cryptographic verification logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain natural language or test framework syntax, not cryptographic APIs. Targeted search found no crypto implementation in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Generated Documentation Loads Third-Party Assets
low_findings.description: The interactive documentation example loads Swagger UI assets from unpkg.com. This is common for demos, but users should pin versions or self-host assets in controlled environments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are explicit CDN references in an HTML documentation template. They do not exfiltrate data by themselves, but they introduce supply-chain and availability considerations.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Findings Are Configuration False Positives
low_findings.description: The env_access hits are dictionary configuration lookups for latency, throughput, error rate, and response size. No evidence found of operating system environment variable reads or secret access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses config.get on a local configuration dictionary. It does not reference process.env, os.environ, dotenv, or similar secret sources.

dangerous_patterns.title: Request Logging Includes Headers And Bodies
dangerous_patterns.description: Mock verification examples collect full headers and JSON bodies. Users should redact secrets and avoid production traffic when using generated mocks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data capture is directly visible in the examples. The security impact is contextual because mock request tracking is also a legitimate testing feature.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked Mock Services And External Documentation Assets
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples start local mock services, fetch localhost endpoints, use curl healthchecks, and load Swagger UI from a CDN. These patterns are expected for API mocks but need environment controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network references are explicit and mostly local. The external CDN reference is real, but no credential transmission or malicious endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often need reliable API behavior before a backend or partner integration is ready. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to design mock servers, scenarios, fixtures, and validation flows.
actual_capabilities: Designs mock server routes for common HTTP methods and dynamic paths.
Defines request matching for path, query, headers, and body conditions.
Creates scenario-based responses for success, errors, latency, and rate limits.
Generates realistic mock data with relationships and schema validation.
Integrates mock servers with Jest, pytest, Docker, and Kubernetes examples.
Produces mock API documentation and interactive documentation templates.
limitations: It does not test live production systems or external partner services.
Generated examples require adaptation before use in regulated environments.
Mock data quality depends on the API contract and fixtures supplied by the user.
It cannot guarantee security of deployed mock infrastructure without review.

use_cases.title: Parallel Frontend Development
use_cases.description: Create stable mock endpoints so frontend engineers can build screens before backend services are complete.

use_cases.title: Contract Validation
use_cases.description: Model expected request and response shapes, then validate generated mocks against OpenAPI-style contracts.

use_cases.title: Demo And Sandbox Environments
use_cases.description: Prepare predictable mock scenarios for sales demos, onboarding flows, and partner integration sandboxes.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Mock
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mock API for these endpoints: [paste endpoints]. Include sample responses, error responses, and simple run instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Matching Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Design request matching rules for this API contract: [paste contract]. Cover path parameters, query parameters, headers, and body variants.

prompt_templates.title: Build Scenario Flows
prompt_templates.prompt: Create mock scenarios for happy path, invalid auth, rate limiting, slow responses, and partial outage for this service: [describe service].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production-Like Mock Infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a secure mock server setup for CI and local development. Include data redaction, contract validation, observability, and deployment checks.

output_examples.input: Mock a user profile API for frontend development.
output_examples.output: A route plan with user lookup, update, and not-found scenarios; deterministic sample users; latency controls; and local run steps.

output_examples.input: Create contract tests for an order service mock.
output_examples.output: A validation checklist covering required fields, status codes, error bodies, request matching, and unmatched request reporting.

output_examples.input: Prepare a demo sandbox for partner onboarding.
output_examples.output: A scenario catalog with successful onboarding, duplicate account, expired token, and service degradation responses.

best_practices: Use synthetic data and redact headers before saving request logs.
Keep mock behavior aligned with a versioned API contract.
Separate local demo mocks from CI mocks and deployed sandbox mocks.
anti_patterns: Do not route production traffic through generated mock servers.
Do not reuse real customer records, tokens, cookies, or partner secrets.
Do not treat mock performance results as proof of production capacity.

faq.question: Can this skill create a working mock server?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides patterns for routes, stubs, scenarios, fixtures, tests, and deployment examples.

faq.question: Does it require a complete API contract?
faq.answer: No, but results improve when you provide endpoints, response schemas, auth behavior, and error formats.

faq.question: Can it integrate with automated tests?
faq.answer: Yes. The playbook includes examples for Jest and pytest style workflows.

faq.question: Can I use real production data in mocks?
faq.answer: No. Use synthetic data and redact sensitive headers, cookies, tokens, and personal information.

faq.question: Does this replace backend integration testing?
faq.answer: No. It helps earlier development and contract validation, but live integration tests are still needed.

faq.question: Why is this skill rated medium risk?
faq.answer: Generated mocks may record headers and bodies, and examples include networked services and external documentation assets.
